title: Bangladesh Garment Exports Cross $50 Billion Mark for First Time in History
summary: The RMG sector's record performance in fiscal 2025-26 cements Bangladesh's position as the world's second largest apparel exporter behind China.
content:
Bangladesh's readymade garment sector has crossed the $50 billion export milestone for the first time in the country's history, according to data released by the Export Promotion Bureau on Sunday, cementing the nation's position as the world's second largest apparel exporter and marking a landmark achievement for an industry that employs over four million workers.

The figure, recorded for fiscal year 2025-26, represents a 12.4 percent increase over the previous year and surpasses the $50 billion target set in the government's export diversification strategy by a margin that surprised even the most optimistic industry projections.

Bangladesh Garment Manufacturers and Exporters Association President Faruque Hassan attributed the achievement to a combination of factors including competitive pricing, improved compliance standards, and the strategic shift toward higher-value product categories such as technical textiles and sustainable fashion lines that have attracted premium buyers from European and North American markets.

Economists noted that the milestone arrives at a time when Bangladesh is navigating the transition away from its Least Developed Country trade preferences, making the sector's underlying competitiveness increasingly important. The government has announced a dedicated $2.8 billion fund to support factory upgrades and skills development over the next three years to sustain momentum through the LDC graduation period.

The achievement was also celebrated as a tribute to Bangladesh's garment workforce, the vast majority of whom are women from rural backgrounds for whom the sector has provided economic mobility unavailable through any other formal employment channel at comparable scale.

title: Dhaka Stock Exchange Hits Record High as Foreign Investment Surges
summary: The DSEX index crossed 7,800 points for the first time, driven by strong earnings from banking and telecom sectors and renewed foreign institutional interest.
content:
The Dhaka Stock Exchange's benchmark DSEX index crossed 7,800 points for the first time on Thursday, driven by strong quarterly earnings from the banking and telecommunications sectors and a significant uptick in foreign institutional investment that market analysts described as a vote of confidence in Bangladesh's medium-term economic trajectory.

The index closed at 7,834 points, a gain of 1.8 percent on the day and a rise of nearly 22 percent since the start of the calendar year, making the DSE one of the best-performing emerging market exchanges in the Asia-Pacific region over the period.

Bangladesh Securities and Exchange Commission Chairman Professor Shibli Rubayat-Ul-Islam called the performance a reflection of genuine improvements in market governance, corporate disclosure standards, and macroeconomic fundamentals. The commission has implemented a series of reforms over the past eighteen months aimed at attracting long-term institutional investors and reducing the market's historic vulnerability to speculative retail trading patterns.

Net foreign purchases on the exchange reached $340 million in the first quarter of 2026, the highest quarterly total in the DSE's history. Analysts at brokerage firm BRAC EPL Securities identified banking sector recapitalization, a stronger taka, and improved current account dynamics as the primary factors drawing international portfolio managers back to Dhaka equities after a period of caution.
